hero.title: 3 Days on Phu Quoc.
hero.titleAccent: Island Hopping, Sea Cable Car & Coral Reefs.
hero.intro: Vietnam's largest island in three compact days. The world's longest three-rope sea cable car across turquoise water to Pineapple Island. Four-island hopping through natural coral reefs. Aquatopia Water Park with 20+ slides. And the Duong Dong Night Market for fresh seafood by the sea at the end of each day.
hero.heroImageAlt: Phu Quoc island hopping speedboat coral reef Vietnam tropical

includes: 4-island hopping speedboat tour with Aquatopia Water Park
World's longest sea cable car return (Hon Thom)
excludes: Dinners (self-arranged at Night Market or local restaurants)
Optional sea walking at Coral Park

overview.heading: 3 Days Phu Quoc: Island Life at an Unbeatable Price
body: This three-day package covers Phu Quoc's two defining activities — island hopping and the Hon Thom cable car — in a compact and affordable format. The island hopping tour on day two covers four islands in the An Thoi Archipelago off Phu Quoc's southern coast: a Pearl Farm, the coral reefs of Coral Park, May Rut Island's white sand beach, Gam Ghi Island's natural coral ecosystems, and Pineapple Island (Hon Thom) with its buffet lunch and Aquatopia Water Park. The return to the mainland is via the Hon Thom Cable Car — 7.9 kilometres across turquoise water, the longest three-rope sea cable car system in the world.

Phu Quoc's beaches and Duong Dong Night Market fill the gaps between the organised tours. Long Beach (Bai Truong) on the west coast faces the sunset — the evening light over the Gulf of Thailand from this beach is exceptional. Duong Dong Night Market, a five-minute walk from most central hotels, offers fresh seafood priced by weight, grilled on the spot, alongside Vietnamese street food and the fish sauce that has made Phu Quoc's name since the 17th century.

For three days, this is the most efficient way to experience Phu Quoc's essential attractions — giving you both the island-hopping coral reef experience and the cable car landmark, with enough free time to enjoy the beaches and food culture that have made this island one of Southeast Asia's most popular destinations.

highlights.heading: Why This 3-Day Phu Quoc Package Stands Apart

items.title: World's Longest Sea Cable Car — Hon Thom
items.body: The Vinpearl Cable Car (Hon Thom Cable Car) holds the Guinness World Record as the longest three-rope cable car system over the sea: 7,899.9 metres from An Thoi at Phu Quoc's southern tip to Hon Thom (Pineapple Island). The crossing takes approximately 15 minutes and passes over open water, with views down to the coral reefs, small islands, and the turquoise Gulf of Thailand water below. The cable car is both the most spectacular way to approach Hon Thom and one of the most photographed experiences in Vietnam.
items.imageAlt: Hon Thom Cable Car Phu Quoc world's longest sea cable car Vietnam

items.title: Four-Island Hopping — Coral Park & Aquatopia
items.body: The An Thoi Archipelago off Phu Quoc's southern coast contains some of the best snorkelling conditions in Vietnam — natural coral ecosystems at 1–3 metres depth, clear water, and minimal boat traffic outside peak season. The four-island tour visits a Pearl Farm (pearls have been farmed in Phu Quoc since the French colonial period), Coral Park for snorkelling (optional sea walking experience), May Rut Island's white sand beach, Gam Ghi Island's 100% natural coral reef, and Pineapple Island (Hon Thom) where Aquatopia Water Park has over 20 slides and attractions. Buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant on Hon Thom is included.
items.imageAlt: Phu Quoc four island hopping coral reef An Thoi Archipelago snorkelling

items.title: Duong Dong Night Market
items.body: The Duong Dong Night Market is Phu Quoc's social centre every evening — the island's fishing boats unload their catch in the morning, and by evening the same fish is grilled and sold by weight from stalls along the market's main alley. The fish sauce stalls, the grilled seafood, the tropical fruit juices, and the general liveliness of the market give a clear picture of what Phu Quoc is actually like when it is not performing for tourists. It is a five-minute walk from most central hotels.
items.imageAlt: Duong Dong Night Market Phu Quoc seafood fish sauce Vietnam

days.title: Arrive Phu Quoc — Free & Easy
days.description: Arrive at Phu Quoc International Airport, met by your Dhesu representative and transferred to your hotel. Check-in and free afternoon to settle in and explore at your own pace. Long Beach (Bai Truong) is within walking distance or a short ride from most central hotels. Evening: Duong Dong Night Market for dinner — fresh seafood priced by weight, Vietnamese street food, and the local fish sauce culture. Ho Quoc Pagoda, the largest temple on the island, is accessible by evening taxi if you want a quieter alternative to the market.
suggestedActivities: Phu Quoc Airport arrival and hotel transfer
Free afternoon to explore at own pace
Optional: Ho Quoc Pagoda evening visit

days.title: 4 Island Hopping & World's Longest Sea Cable Car
days.meals: Breakfast, Lunch on Hon Thom Island
days.description: 08:00 comfortable shuttle pickup from hotel. Transfer to An Thoi Harbour for the speedboat island-hopping tour. Visit the Pearl Farm (Phu Quoc pearls have been cultured since the colonial period). Coral Park for snorkelling — optional sea walking experience (paid extra). May Rut Island for its white sand beach, crystal-clear water, and flycam photography. Gam Ghi Island's 100% natural coral ecosystem in 1–3m of water. Noon: Arrive at Hon Thom (Pineapple Island) for buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant (100+ European and Asian dishes). Afternoon: Aquatopia Water Park's 20+ slides, the Eagle Eye Observatory for panoramic island views, and beach relaxation. 16:00: Scenic return to Phu Quoc via the world's longest sea cable car. 17:00: Shuttle back to hotel.
suggestedActivities: Gam Ghi Island natural coral reef
Return via Hon Thom Cable Car

days.title: Phu Quoc Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Phu Quoc for self-arranged activities — Sao Beach on the southeast coast (regularly rated among the best beaches in Southeast Asia, with fine white sand and shallow, calm water), the Dinh Cau Rock Temple at the northern end of Long Beach, or the Pepper Farm and Fish Sauce Factory in the north of the island. Transfer to Phu Quoc International Airport per your agreed schedule.
suggestedActivities: Optional: Pepper Farm or Fish Sauce Factory

We arrange the island hopping, cable car, hotel, and airport transfers so you can arrive and immediately start enjoying the island.

sections.heading: 3 Day Phu Quoc Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 3-day Phu Quoc tour from Malaysia is the most popular format for Malaysian visitors to Vietnam's largest island — short enough to fit into a long weekend (Malaysia to Phu Quoc is approximately 2 hours by direct flight), comprehensive enough to cover both of Phu Quoc's headline attractions: the world's longest sea cable car and the An Thoi Archipelago island hopping with coral reef snorkelling.

Malaysian passport holders can enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days, making Phu Quoc one of the easiest international beach destinations to access from Malaysia. No advance visa application required.

sections.heading: Is 3 Days Enough for Phu Quoc?
sections.body: Three days is enough to experience Phu Quoc's two essential activities — the island hopping and the cable car — and enjoy the beaches and night market food culture. For a more comprehensive visit that includes the north of the island (Pepper Farm, Fish Sauce Factory, Ho Quoc Pagoda) and a dedicated Sao Beach day, the 5-day Best of Phu Quoc package is recommended. The 3-day Super Saver is ideal for first-time visitors and those with limited time who want the island's headline experiences.

faq.heading: Questions About This 3-Day Phu Quoc Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Arrive at Phu Quoc International Airport with your Malaysian passport — no advance application required.

items.question: Is the Hon Thom Cable Car included?
items.answer: Yes. The Hon Thom Cable Car return journey is included in the island hopping package (Day 2). You go to Pineapple Island via speedboat and return via the cable car — the most scenic direction of travel for the return leg, with 15 minutes across open water looking back at Phu Quoc.

items.question: Can I see starfish on the island hopping?
items.answer: Starfish are visible at Gam Ghi Island and May Rut Island during the October-to-March season, when sea conditions are calm and visibility is best. Outside this season, the starfish are still present but may be less visible due to current and visibility conditions.

items.question: What is the best beach on Phu Quoc?
items.answer: Sao Beach on the southeast coast is consistently rated the most beautiful beach on Phu Quoc — fine white sand, shallow calm water, and a quieter atmosphere than Long Beach. It is best visited in the morning before tour group boats arrive. Long Beach (Bai Truong) on the west coast is more accessible from most hotels and has better sunset views. Both are worth visiting if your schedule allows.

items.question: What should I eat in Phu Quoc?
items.answer: Fresh seafood is the answer — specifically at Duong Dong Night Market, where the day's catch is grilled to order and sold by weight. Phu Quoc fish sauce (nuoc mam Phu Quoc), made from anchovies fermented for 12 months, is the most famous food product from the island and is available at the market and fish sauce factories. Grilled scallops with spring onion and peanut oil (so diep nuong) are a local specialty.
